Title: Unsigned SAML LogoutRequest Acceptance in gosaml2
Description: The `ValidateEncodedLogoutRequestPOST` function in gosaml2 accepts completely unsigned SAML `LogoutRequest` messages even when `SkipSignatureValidation` is set to `false`. When `validateElementSignature` returns `dsig.ErrMissingSignature`, the code in `decode_logout_request.go:60-62` silently falls through to process the unverified XML element instead of rejecting it. An attacker who can reach the SP's Single Logout endpoint can forge a `LogoutRequest` for any user, terminating their session without possessing the IdP's signing key.
